svg.text: Overlapping pod CIDRs (100.64.0.0/16) · Spoke-GW SNAT hides the overlap · Direct internet egress, no Cloud NAT · GKE Gateway (Global ALB) handles ingress out-of-band — Blueprint Architecture

g.text: GCP Edge · Google-managed Global External ALB plane (out-of-band of Aviatrix data path)
frontend GW IP, reserved · NEG → Gatus
backend GW IP, reserved · NEG → Gatus
east-west calls Backend Gatus via spoke→transit→spoke
PSC peering to nodes · K8s API plane
DCF metadata discovery via Aviatrix Controller
Three policies on this spoke GW:
• pods 100.64.0.0/16 → transit fabric → SNAT to GW priv IP
• pods 100.64.0.0/16 → internet (eth0) → SNAT to GW pub IP
• nodes 10.10.0.0/22 → internet (eth0) → SNAT to GW pub IP
DCF SmartGroups still see original pod IP pre-NAT
east-west calls Frontend Gatus via spoke→transit→spoke
PSC peering to nodes · K8s API plane
DCF metadata discovery via Aviatrix Controller
Three policies on this spoke GW:
• pods 100.64.0.0/16 → transit fabric → SNAT to GW priv IP
• pods 100.64.0.0/16 → internet (eth0) → SNAT to GW pub IP
• nodes 10.20.0.0/22 → internet (eth0) → SNAT to GW pub IP
DCF SmartGroups still see original pod IP pre-NAT
B · east-west: pod 100.64.x.x → SNAT 10.10.4.2 → IPsec → spoke → pod

svg.text: VPC route: 0.0.0.0/0 → spoke GW (priority 991, untagged) — Controller-managed
VPC route: 0.0.0.0/0 → spoke GW (priority 991, untagged) — Controller-managed

g.text: DCF Policy Enforcement (fabric-wide · evaluated at every spoke gateway)
SmartGroups are NAT-aware: rules match the original pod IP (100.64.x.x) before spoke-GW SNAT. Cluster onboarding tags pods by namespace + service via the K8s-firewall agent.
